waveDependencies.note: Waves 0 and 1 can run in parallel. No inter-wave dependencies.

verificationGate.policy: All four must pass before advancing to Waves 2-3.

testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Convex mutation determinism — eliminate Date.now() inside mutations
tasks.description: Convex mutations must be deterministic because they replay on retry. Multiple mutations call Date.now() directly or via optional fallback (args.now ?? Date.now()). Fix: (1) In convex/foodParsing.ts (processEvidence, ~L1551,L1570): make `now` a required arg, remove ?? Date.now() fallback. Update all callers (processLogInternal scheduler call) to pass `now: Date.now()`. (2) In convex/migrations.ts (normalizeProfilesV2, ~L1146): replace Date.now() with a required `now` arg. (3) In convex/foodRequests.ts (submitRequest, ~L42): make `now` required, remove ?? fallback. (4) In shared/foodEvidence.ts (buildFoodEvidenceResult, ~L1213): make `args.now` required so callers must supply timestamp. (5) For client-supplied timestamps in src/contexts/ProfileContext.tsx, src/hooks/usePendingReplies.ts, src/hooks/useAiInsights.ts: remove `now`/`timestamp` from mutation arg schemas; generate authoritative timestamp server-side in the mutation handler. Search for all Date.now() in convex/ non-test files to ensure none remain in mutation handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'Date.now()' convex/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v test | grep -v node_modules | grep -v _generated returns zero matches inside mutation/internalMutation handlers
All `now` args in mutation validators are v.number() (not v.optional)
All scheduler calls (ctx.scheduler.runAfter) pass now: Date.now() as an argument
No client-side code passes `timestamp` or `now` to mutation args (removed from schemas)
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass (update test fixtures to pass required `now` arg)

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Cross-tenant conversation query — add compound index
tasks.description: conversations.listByReport fetches all messages for a given aiAnalysisId without userId scoping at the DB layer, then filters in-memory. Fix: (1) In convex/schema.ts, add a compound index to the conversations table: `by_userId_aiAnalysisId: ['userId', 'aiAnalysisId']`. (2) In convex/conversations.ts (~L90-L103), replace the by_aiAnalysisId index query with the compound index: `.withIndex('by_userId_aiAnalysisId', q => q.eq('userId', userId).eq('aiAnalysisId', args.aiAnalysisId)).collect()`. Remove the in-memory userId filter since the index now enforces it. Read convex/_generated/ai/guidelines.md first for Convex patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: convex/schema.ts conversations table has by_userId_aiAnalysisId compound index
listByReport uses the compound index with both userId and aiAnalysisId
No in-memory userId filter remains in listByReport
Typecheck passes
All existing conversation tests pass

tasks.title: Remove hardcoded patient name 'Peter' from AI prompts
tasks.description: src/lib/aiAnalysis.ts (~L1316,L1320) contains hardcoded references to 'Peter' including personal ADHD/eating details in the LLM system prompt. This is a PII violation and breaks the 'No Hard-Coding Personalization' rule. Fix: Replace all 'Peter' references with the existing `preferredName` field from AiPreferences (already inserted via sanitizeNameForPrompt) or the generic 'the patient'. Search the entire codebase for any other hardcoded 'Peter' references in prompt-building code. Note: W0-04 and W0-05 also edit this file — those tasks depend on this one completing first.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -rn '"Peter"' src/lib/aiAnalysis.ts returns zero matches
grep -rn 'Peter' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v test | grep -v node_modules shows no prompt-building references to 'Peter'
All name references in prompt use preferredName variable or 'the patient'
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds

tasks.title: Add per-field length caps for health profile fields in AI prompts
tasks.description: Free-text health profile fields (medications, supplements, allergies, intolerances, lifestyleNotes, dietaryHistory, otherConditions) are embedded in the LLM system prompt with only a 50,000-char global limit — far too high per field and vulnerable to prompt injection. Fix: In src/lib/aiAnalysis.ts (~L1009-L1058), add per-field length caps at the prompt-building layer: medications/supplements/allergies 500 chars each, lifestyleNotes/dietaryHistory 1000 chars each, otherConditions 200 chars. Apply the same sanitizeNameForPrompt-style treatment (strip BiDi characters, HTML tags) to all free-text fields, not just preferredName. Create a helper like `sanitizeProfileField(value: string, maxLen: number): string` that truncates and sanitizes.
acceptanceCriteria: Each health profile field has an explicit maxLength cap (medications: 500, supplements: 500, allergies: 500, lifestyleNotes: 1000, dietaryHistory: 1000, otherConditions: 200)
All free-text fields are sanitized (BiDi chars, HTML tags stripped) before prompt embedding
A field exceeding its cap is truncated, not rejected
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Strip XML delimiters from sanitizeNameForPrompt
tasks.description: sanitizeNameForPrompt in src/lib/aiAnalysis.ts (~L51-L59) does not strip < and > characters. A name like 'Pete</patient_name>INJECTED<patient_name>' would close the XML tag early and inject into the prompt. Fix: Add .replace(/[<>]/g, '') to sanitizeNameForPrompt alongside the existing sanitization. This is a one-line addition.
acceptanceCriteria: sanitizeNameForPrompt strips < and > characters from input
A name containing '<script>' or '</tag>' has those characters removed

tasks.title: Sanitize AI error messages before rendering in UI
tasks.description: AI pipeline errors are rendered verbatim in AnalysisProgressOverlay.tsx (~L40-L57) and surfaced from useAiInsights.ts (~L354-L357). These can contain API keys, request IDs, model names, rate-limit quotas. Fix: (1) Create a formatAiError(err: unknown): string helper (in src/lib/aiErrorFormatter.ts or near useApiKey.ts which already has sanitizeApiKeyError). Map known OpenAI error classes: rate limit → 'Too many requests, try again later', auth → 'API key problem', timeout → 'Request timed out', generic → 'Analysis failed'. Strip anything matching sk-... pattern. (2) In useAiInsights.ts, replace raw getErrorMessage(err) with formatAiError(err). (3) In AnalysisProgressOverlay.tsx, ensure the 'Show more' toggle also renders sanitized output.
acceptanceCriteria: formatAiError helper exists and maps known error types to user-friendly strings
Strings matching sk-... pattern are stripped from error output
useAiInsights uses formatAiError instead of raw getErrorMessage
AnalysisProgressOverlay renders only sanitized error text
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds

tasks.title: Add rehype-sanitize to AI markdown rendering
tasks.description: react-markdown renders AI-generated content in ConversationPanel.tsx (~L133,L182) and DrPooReport.tsx (~L93,L129,L169,L183) without rehype-sanitize. A prompt injection attack could emit malicious markdown/HTML. Fix: (1) Install rehype-sanitize: `bun add rehype-sanitize`. (2) Add rehypePlugins={[rehypeSanitize]} to all react-markdown instances rendering AI content. (3) Add a MAX_AI_CONTENT_CHARS = 4000 constant and slice AI strings before passing to <Markdown>. Log a warning when content is truncated.
acceptanceCriteria: rehype-sanitize is in package.json dependencies
All react-markdown instances rendering AI content include rehypePlugins={[rehypeSanitize]}
AI content strings are sliced to MAX_AI_CONTENT_CHARS (4000) before rendering
Content exceeding the limit logs a console.warn
Typecheck passes
Build succeeds

tasks.title: Clamp ADD_TO_STAGING portion to MAX_PORTION_G
tasks.description: In useNutritionStore.ts (~L243-L244), the ADD_TO_STAGING reducer case increments portionG without clamping to MAX_PORTION_G (500g). Rapid (+) tapping can push above 500g, corrupting calorie/macro data. The ADJUST_STAGING_PORTION case already clamps correctly. Fix: One-line change in ADD_TO_STAGING case: `const newPortionG = Math.min(existing.portionG + increment, MAX_PORTION_G);` then use newPortionG in the updated item.
acceptanceCriteria: ADD_TO_STAGING case clamps result to MAX_PORTION_G
Rapid (+) tapping on a staged item cannot exceed 500g
Typecheck passes
Existing nutrition store tests pass

tasks.title: Fix double-submit vulnerability in FoodSection & BowelSection
tasks.description: FoodSection.tsx (~L32-L54) and BowelSection.tsx (~L206) guard with `if (saving) return` then setSaving(true), but React 18 concurrent rendering can allow two rapid invocations to both pass the guard before either state update commits. Fix: (1) In both components, add a useRef as immediate non-reactive guard: `const submittingRef = useRef(false); if (submittingRef.current) return; submittingRef.current = true;`. Reset in finally block. (2) In BowelSection, also wrap handleSave in useCallback to prevent stale closures.
acceptanceCriteria: Both FoodSection and BowelSection use useRef as submit guard
submittingRef.current is reset in a finally block after async operation
BowelSection handleSave is wrapped in useCallback
Double-rapid-click cannot create duplicate log entries
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Validate timestamp range in usePanelTime
tasks.description: usePanelTime (~L17-L35) accepts user-entered date strings with no range validation. Far-future or epoch-0 timestamps break queries and transit calculations. Fix: In getTimestampMs(), after parsing the date string, clamp the result: no earlier than 5 years ago (Date.now() - 5 * 365.25 * 86400000), no later than tomorrow (Date.now() + 86400000). If out of range, return the current timestamp and optionally log a warning.
acceptanceCriteria: Timestamps earlier than 5 years ago are clamped
Timestamps later than tomorrow are clamped
Valid timestamps within range pass through unchanged
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Add Bristol code integer validation
tasks.description: Bristol Stool Scale codes are 1-7 integers only, but the pipeline accepts non-integer floats (7.9), negative numbers (-5), and out-of-range values (8, 99). Fix: (1) In shared/foodEvidence.ts buildDigestiveEvents (~L416-L418): add guard `if (bristolCode !== undefined && (!Number.isInteger(bristolCode) || bristolCode < 1 || bristolCode > 7)) continue;`. (2) In shared/logDataParsers.ts (~L58-L62): add the same integer+range check after safeNumber coercion for bristolCode. (3) In normalizeDigestiveCategory (~L446-L449): ensure bristol >= 7 means exactly 7, not 8+.
acceptanceCriteria: Bristol code 0, -1, -5, 8, 99, 7.5, 7.9 are all rejected/skipped
Bristol codes 1-7 (integers only) pass validation
normalizeDigestiveCategory does not categorize bristol > 7

tasks.title: Move AI rate limiting server-side
tasks.description: The client-side rate limiter in src/lib/aiRateLimiter.ts resets on page reload and is shared across unrelated AI features. Fix: (1) In the relevant Convex actions (ai.ts and habitCoaching Convex action), enforce per-user cooldowns server-side. Store lastCallAt per user per feature type (e.g., 'drpoo' vs 'coaching') in a Convex document or check existing timestamp fields. Reject calls that violate the 5-minute interval. (2) Use separate rate limit keys for different AI call types so coaching doesn't block Dr. Poo. (3) Remove the dead `if (MIN_CALL_INTERVAL_MS <= 0) return;` branch from aiRateLimiter.ts. (4) Keep the client-side check as a UX guard only (not security-critical). Read convex/_generated/ai/guidelines.md first.
acceptanceCriteria: Server-side rate limiting enforced in Convex action (per user, per feature type)

tasks.description: foodSafetyUtils.ts exports FILTER_OPTIONS, SortKey, SortDir — TanStack Table uses its own types. Fix: (1) grep for import sites to verify zero usage. (2) Delete the exports. (3) Remove corresponding barrel entries from database/index.ts. Note: W1-05 already removes some dead exports from this file — this task handles the remaining ones that need verification first.
acceptanceCriteria: FILTER_OPTIONS, SortKey, SortDir not exported from any file
No import-site breakage (grep verified zero imports)
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Remove barrel re-export indirection in food modules
tasks.description: shared/foodCanonicalization.ts re-exports functions that belong to other modules, creating 3-level import indirection. shared/foodProjection.ts also re-exports resolveCanonicalFoodName. Fix: (1) Remove re-exports from foodCanonicalization.ts — keep only canonicalizeKnownFoodName and its direct helpers. (2) Remove resolveCanonicalFoodName re-export from foodProjection.ts. (3) Update all consumers to import from the owning module directly. (4) Ensure PortionData is importable from exactly one canonical location.
acceptanceCriteria: foodCanonicalization.ts exports only its own functions (canonicalizeKnownFoodName etc.)
foodProjection.ts does not re-export resolveCanonicalFoodName
PortionData importable from exactly one location
All consumers import from the owning module
Typecheck passes
